GT LEARN #943 🧬 Find the Shortest Superstring HARD Bitmask DP TSP String
words= exp: "cat...act" (len 5)

📝 Problem Statement

Given an array words (1 ≤ words.length ≤ 12), return the shortest string that contains every word as a substring. If multiple answers exist, return any. Words may overlap (e.g. "abc" + "bcd" → "abcd", saving 2 chars). This is the classic Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP) in disguise — visit all "cities" (words) with maximum overlap.

🧠 Solving Approach — Bitmask DP (Held-Karp)

Three phases: (1) precompute overlap[i][j] = max chars of words[j]'s prefix that match words[i]'s suffix; (2) DP where dp[mask][i] = max total overlap when mask bits are picked and last picked is i; (3) reconstruct path via parent[mask][i] and stitch the words.
🎯 Bitmask state: mask is an n-bit integer; bit i = 1 means words[i] already used. 2ⁿ × n states total. For n=4: 16 masks × 4 endings = 64 cells.
♻️ Transition: dp[mask | (1<<i)][i] = max(dp[mask][j] + overlap[j][i]) for all j already in mask. We pick the previous endpoint j that gives max overlap.
🏁 Reconstruct: Find the full_mask ending i with max total overlap, then walk parent[mask][i] backwards to recover the order.
⏱ Time: O(2ⁿ · n²) 💾 Space: O(2ⁿ · n)

🧪 Test Cases

T13 words, anagrams — overlap chain→ len 5
["cat","tac","act"]
T23 words, perfect 2-char chain→ "abcde"
["abc","bcd","cde"]
T33 words, 1-char overlap each→ "alexlovesleetcode"-ish
["alex","loves","leetcode"]
T4tiny — 2 words→ "aba"/"bab"
["ab","ba"]
T53 words — clean chain xy→yz→za→ "xyza"
["xy","yz","za"]
Output: —
0/0
←→R 100%
